title: erigeron atticus var. atticus
intro: Erigeron atticus var. atticus, commonly known as Atticus fleabane, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the eastern United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Erigeron atticus var. atticus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote overall health and well-being.
